title: TFIND-013: Output journal / A4 path needed modeling
description:
Area: output journal.

Status: resolved (#338, 960ec59b3 — output-journal-pipeline.ts toy config landed).

Triage: cat-4/toy (toy A4/output-journal modeling = the #338 toy config; the A4 prod path itself is tracked separately as residue).

Halt-class clearance recorded: an interim CI-red showed 4th per-context output row ([0,1,2,3] vs [0,1,2]) on #318/#331 AgentOutputAfter path — investigated as possible duplicate-output regression. Root cause was test-fixture double-emit (toy agent fixture printed Terminated envelope and stdio-jsonl codec derives terminal evidence from process exit ⇒ two terminal rows); fixed by fixture no longer printing Terminated. NOT a #318/#331 production regression — deterministic fixture bug.
close_reason: Resolved by #338 — output-journal-pipeline.ts landed; A4 fixture bug also caught.

title: TFIND-035: Two divergent agent-output envelope decoders (SSOT consolidation)
description:
Area: protocol / runtime SSOT.

Status: resolved (#333, 28aac646f).

Triage: cat-2 (SSOT/boundary — two divergent envelope decoders; defensible as TFIND-030's tracked completion).

Surfaced during TFIND-030. Two envelope decoders for agent-output rows: runtime's (already typed) and protocol's (previously Record). TFIND-030 Option C deliberately left runtime decoder and runtime-owned AgentOutputEventSchema in place to keep blast radius minimal.

Resolution: canonical AgentOutputEvent union + part sub-schemas relocated to @firegrid/protocol/agent-output subpath (Q3); @firegrid/runtime contract.ts re-exports moved subset, dup defs deleted (Q1=A relocate+re-export); one envelope decoder with two observation shapes retained (Q2). Completion signal: scoped .jscpd.json ignore DELETED — duplication genuinely eliminated, not hidden.
close_reason: Resolved by #333 — canonical AgentOutputEvent union relocated to @firegrid/protocol; scoped jscpd ignore deleted.

title: TFIND-001: Client launch handle is not a session handle
description:
Area: client-sdk.

Status: resolved (#332 — folded into the consolidated client/host transaction).

Triage: cat-1 (real client consumer hits launch/session split; folded into the #332 cluster).

Firegrid.launch() created a RuntimeContextHandle with contextId and snapshot, but programmatic prompt/start/wait operations were exposed on FiregridSessionHandle from sessions.createOrLoad / sessions.attach. The #332 client/host transaction resolves this: launch is now the same durable-request seam as sessions.createOrLoad, no longer host-bound.

Resolution: #332 (2d474c70d).
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — folded into the consolidated client/host transaction.

title: TFIND-002: Session creation still required host identity
description:
Area: client-sdk / host boundary.

Status: resolved (#332, 2d474c70d).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — client-only frontend hits host-identity wall immediately).

Firegrid.sessions.createOrLoad required CurrentHostSession because it created a host-bound RuntimeContext row through insertLocalRuntimeContext. That made remote-client-shaped tests impossible through the public session creation API: the client had to be composed with host identity, even though a production client should not be in the same Effect environment as a host.

CLUSTER RESOLUTION (Gurdas 2026-05-18, #332 merged — consolidated client/host boundary, Option 1):
- sessions.createOrLoad writes a RuntimeContextRequestRow (full public runtime intent) — no CurrentHostSession on the client path.
- Folded TFIND-001/003/038/039 into the same transaction.
- session.start writes a durable RuntimeStartRequestRow; host-side control-request-reconciler binds contexts + claims/runs starts.
- RuntimeContextRequestRow carries argv/env/agent protocol/MCP — arbitrary public runtime intent expressible client-side.
- CLI/factory migrated to host-owned synchronous startRuntime same-transaction (no compat wait-helper).
- Abandon semantics = terminal/no-revival/10min, idempotent claim via insertOrGet, duplicate-start suppression — guarded by 5 named deterministic failure-mode tests + 6th determinism test; CI-confirmed green; no cast on reconciler/claim/dedup/completion path.
- TFIND-004/008 unblocked: durable-intent/reconciler seam makes separate-process client/host e2e expressible.
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — consolidated client/host boundary, Option 1 (client writes durable intent; host reconciler claims starts).

title: TFIND-036: Firegrid MCP toolkit lacked a read-only runtime-state query tool
description:
Area: MCP / tools.

Status: RE-TRIAGED cat-3 → toy redirect (2026-05-18; was QUEUED-FOR-ARCHITECT/SDD #335).

Triage: cat-3 (rubric CANONICAL — 'agent reads its own runtime exit code' has NO coherent non-Firegrid consumer; test-fixture awkwardness. NO production code shipped — caught at SDD stage).

The triage question: would a real consumer outside Firegrid need an agent to read its own runtime-run exit code? No — the agent is inside the current run (it has not exited), and runtime-run exit/signal is host-plane forensic data with no coherent in-run agent use. The sleep durationMs:0 'workaround' was the toy reaching for a plausible-sounding capability to exercise the MCP bridge — test-fixture awkwardness wearing capability clothes.

Toy fix: exercise the MCP bridge with an existing tool, or use wait_for{RuntimeRun} where the config genuinely needs run presence / terminal state.

PROCESS NOTE: TFIND-036 reached a 4th-revision SDD before this triage was applied — the process gap that motivated the rubric. It was caught at SDD stage; no production code shipped.
close_reason: Closed cat-3 — no coherent non-Firegrid consumer; toy redirect.

title: TFIND-043: DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine VALIDATION.5 flakes under 17-way local turbo
description:
Area: runtime / test infra.

Status: open (low priority — test-infra flake, TFIND-042-class, not a regression).

Triage: cat-4 (test-infra/tooling artifact — load-contention flake; CI-arbiter, correctly NOT dispatched to a production sidecar).

Surfaced during #326 fork(1) verification. PASSES isolated (~509ms) but flakes under 17-way local turbo contention. Distinct test from TFIND-042 (that was scenario-firegrid CLI --help) but same class: load-induced contention, not a determinism/correctness defect.

title: TFIND-030: Snapshot agent output events were typed as records, not protocol unions
description:
Area: client-sdk / projections.

Status: resolved (#329, a7c76c268).

Triage: cat-1 (real client-sdk consumer wants the typed AgentOutputEvent union from snapshot without casts).

The durable-streams-backed test needed a local textDeltas projection that checked agentOutputs[].event as Record<string, unknown> instead of the typed AgentOutputEvent union. The runtime output rows were decoded correctly at runtime, but the client snapshot projection type lost the discriminated event shape.

Resolution: protocol-owned AgentOutputEvent union (byte-mirroring runtime via the same @effect/ai Prompt/Response primitives; @effect/ai added to @firegrid/protocol — Gurdas-blessed, lint:deps clean). Protocol envelope/observation decode parses event against the union; session.snapshot().agentOutputs[].event is the typed union, re-exported from client-sdk. Q2 STRICT: non-conforming event → Option.none.
close_reason: Resolved by #329 — protocol-owned AgentOutputEvent union; SSOT consolidation tracked as TFIND-035.

title: TFIND-046: Client SDK exposes ControlPlane tag but not its stream-URL builder
description:
Area: client-sdk / durable tables.

Status: open (low priority — client-sdk ergonomic completeness; annotated in #341 MIGRATION_NOTES).

Triage: cat-1 (real consumer gap — a client-side live control-plane query, the Flamecast useDurableTable pattern, must reach into protocol for the stream URL; low severity but consumer-facing; fix = client-sdk re-export/fold. NOT toy-test-cleanness: a real non-Firegrid consumer hits the identical import).

Client SDK exposes FiregridRuntimeTables.ControlPlane (the durable table tag), but to instantiate its layer a consumer must supply the stream URL via runtimeControlPlaneStreamUrl, which is only exported from @firegrid/protocol/launch. So consumer-shaped code that builds a client-side live query over the control-plane table (the Flamecast useDurableTable(FiregridRuntimeTables.ControlPlane) + live-query pattern) must reach past the client SDK into a protocol URL helper.

Next action: re-export runtimeControlPlaneStreamUrl from @firegrid/client-sdk so consumer-shaped code does not import protocol URL helpers directly.

title: TFIND-028: RuntimeStartCapabilityLive did not capture workflow support services
description:
Area: host-sdk / runtime start.

Status: resolved (#325).

Triage: cat-1 (REAL production runtime bug — an operator running a host via the public capability hits a missing RuntimeOutputTable; surfaced by the toy adopting prod composition).

Switching the durable-streams-backed configuration to production FiregridRuntimeHostLive surfaced that RuntimeStartCapabilityLive captured RuntimeContextEngineRegistry and AgentToolHost, but not host-scoped services needed later by runtimeContextWorkflowSupportLayer. Calling RuntimeStartCapability.start as a public host capability failed at runtime with missing RuntimeOutputTable. Fix captures full host context.
close_reason: Resolved by #325 — full host context captured.

title: TFIND-039: Client SDK had no client-visible runtime start trigger
description:
Area: client-sdk / host split.

Status: resolved (#332 — durable start trigger + host reconciler).

Triage: cat-1 (real client needs a durable start trigger; cluster end-state, folded into #332).

The Codex ACP tool-call test manually extracted RuntimeStartCapability from host context and called start({ contextId }). The client SDK had no durable start request or client-visible way to ask a host to start a runtime context.
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — durable RuntimeStartRequestRow + host-side control-request-reconciler.

title: config: codex-acp-tool-call-pipeline

title: TFIND-048: MCP route + URL lifecycle ownership unresolved in #332 model
description:
Area: client/host boundary / MCP route + URL lifecycle.

Status: open — REFRAMED 2026-05-18 (Reading 2 / architectural; was mis-shaped as 'client-sdk missing helper'); framing-gated, folds into the #332-impl MCP-lifecycle question; blocks Codex ACP.

Triage: cat-1/2 architectural (real production-model gap, not a missing API: re-exporting sessionContextIdForExternalKey would canonize the backwards lifecycle = WRONG fix. The determinism primitive itself is sound; the smell is URL-lifecycle ownership. Migration-as-validation working exactly as designed — proved #332 left an MCP-lifecycle hole. NOT cat-3 toy-fix).

The #332 client/host model does not resolve who builds the concrete contextId-scoped MCP URL and when. Codex ACP bakes it client-side pre-createOrLoad; production (host-sdk/mcp-host.ts, CLI) has the host own the MCP server with a /mcp/runtime-context/:contextId route resolved at tool-call time. Client-baking a concrete URL into the intent is test-fixture-shaped.

Evidence (file:line):
- sessionContextIdForExternalKey (protocol/src/session-facade/schema.ts:482) is sound deterministic primitive for client/host independent reconciliation, NOT for clients to pre-bake route URLs.
- Production host owns MCP server: host-sdk/src/host/mcp-host.ts:1-26 mounts /mcp/runtime-context/:contextId; route param is request authority, resolved at tool-call time (:118-124), no pre-provisioning.
- Codex ACP test/config builds concrete URL pre-createOrLoad and embeds in intent — test fixture starts server before context exists.
- SDD_CONSOLIDATED_CLIENT_HOST_BOUNDARY.md:49-95 is silent on who builds the concrete contextId-scoped MCP URL and when.
- No other config uses MCP or pre-derives contextId-scoped URL. Codex ACP is unique.

Resolution path: architectural framing decision on MCP route/URL lifecycle in #332 model. Codex ACP stays paused/unmigrated; resumed migration is the validation.

The toy has an in-memory wait-for-output configuration, but durable-streams-backed exercises client wait.forAgentOutput through output table only for modeled text path. Does not yet model runtime durable-tools wait_for surface or production wait router.

description:
Area: tiny host coverage.

Status: resolved (#325).

Triage: cat-4/toy (toy fidelity — toy should compose production host; no production surface gap; its adoption surfaced cat-1 TFIND-028).

The durable-streams-backed configuration used real Durable Streams + DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine, but its host side was still a tiny RuntimeStartCapability with in-memory active-engine registry and tiny AgentSessionService. #325 replaces with FiregridRuntimeHostLive (production registry, dispatcher, runtime workflow session, per-context output writer, tool-host support, durable-tools observation substrate).
close_reason: Resolved by #325 — toy adopts FiregridRuntimeHostLive; surfaced cat-1 TFIND-028.

title: TFIND-038: Client session creation cannot express arbitrary runtime intents
description:
Area: client-sdk / runtime config.

Status: resolved (#332 — RuntimeContextRequest carries full public runtime intent).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — real consumer launching a specific agent binary + MCP hits the client-intent gap).

The Codex ACP tool-call test manually constructed a RuntimeContext with makeLocalRuntimeContextForHostSession and wrote it through RuntimeControlPlaneTable.contexts.upsert. It did this because the client session facade couldn't create/load a session with arbitrary runtime configuration: binary argv, env bindings, ACP protocol selection, MCP server declarations.

TOY-REALIZATION DECISION (Gurdas 2026-05-18) — option (3): migrate BOTH Codex ACP and stdio-jsonl tests to the production-fidelity public client/session surface in ONE PR by ONE agent (toy maintainer), where migration == validation. stdio-jsonl migration landed (#343); Codex ACP migration paused on TFIND-048.
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — RuntimeContextRequest carries full public runtime intent.

title: TFIND-005: Workflow layer composition leaks type precision (KEYSTONE)
description:
Area: Effect layer typing.

Status: #326 VERIFIED (Crux-B both arms pass; strict scope; gates green), NOT flip-ready — co-gated on TFIND-044 + TFIND-045 + TFIND-050.

Triage: cat-2 (rubric anchor — any leaks Layer precision through public host factories; type-honesty boundary).

Composing Workflow.toLayer, DurableTable.layer, and DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine.layer leaks any through Layer pipe inference even when every consumed service is named explicitly.

FRAMING SIGNED OFF (Gurdas 2026-05-18): curried (ns,schemas)<Self>() shape accepted; options-object / .tag<Self>() rejected. The SDD proved minimal fix inert and naive precise fix type-unsound (cross-table Identifier unification). Implementation on #326; zero runtime/behavior change.

EXECUTION VERIFIED (Agent 2, 2026-05-18): Strict idiom migration on 15af74c4e. Crux-B binding condition MET (both probe arms pass); strict scope confirmed (7 prod + 14 test idiom appends); gates green (lint + tests). Flip HARD-GATED on flamecast typecheck — co-gated on TFIND-044 (flamecast main.tsx:360) and TFIND-045 (host-sdk reconciler env leak).

Keystone cascade: TFIND-007-step2 + TFIND-029 (#328) unblock on #326 merge.

title: TFIND-016: Activity boundaries directly modeled (toy)
description:
Area: workflow activities.

Status: resolved (cat-3 CLOSE 2026-05-18, Gurdas-concurred).

Triage: cat-3 (test-fixture-shaped — modeling an internal-by-design abstraction directly is the wrong shape; redirect = observe activity-mediated behavior via the public surface, already covered).

A dedicated activity-boundary config necessarily exercises an INTERNAL abstraction (Activity.make), violating the CONFIGS 'drive examples through public boundaries' rule. Activity-mediated durability (replay/retry) is observed through the public surface and already covered by the durable-streams replay config. No new config; no production change.
close_reason: Closed cat-3 — activity-boundary modeling violates public-surface rule; redirect to durable-streams replay observation.

title: TFIND-040: Client SDK lacks a per-event session observation surface
description:
Area: client-sdk / observations.

Status: FULLY DECIDED (Gurdas 2026-05-18) — Q1=A attach at SESSION level, Q2=live-tail-until-scope-close, Q3=subscribe-only-first, Q4=generic-stream-only. SDD #334 SDD_FIREGRID_SESSION_OBSERVATION_SURFACE.md. Impl ready to dispatch (queued behind keystone).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — real client consumer pattern: session.subscribe per-event; correctly SDD-first).

The substrate primitive already exists internally (waitForAgentOutputObservation = RuntimeOutputTable.events.rows() tail → filterMap → filter); subscribe is that pipeline minus Stream.runHead, yielding TFIND-030 typed RuntimeAgentOutputObservation. No substrate/protocol change; wait.forAgentOutput refactors to subscribe |> filter |> runHead (one code path, no drift).

Projection-scope clarification (Gurdas Q): the observation element RuntimeAgentOutputObservation is a protocol projection so SEMANTICS are plane-neutral and would carry — BUT TFIND-040 decided the CLIENT binding only; an agent-tool binding of subscribe is a SEPARATE, not-yet-made decision. Client impl MUST keep observation contract binding-neutral so a future symmetric agent-tool binding is not foreclosed.

title: TFIND-047: Snapshot agentOutputs typing weaker than runtime observation type
description:
Area: client-sdk / snapshot observation typing.

Status: open (filed 2026-05-18 by Gurdas; framing-gated, distinct from TFIND-040).

Triage: cat-2 (real client-SDK type-precision boundary — the snapshot path loses observation type precision the runtime side has, forcing consumer casts; distinct from TFIND-040 subscription ergonomics; relates TFIND-030/035 SSOT but a deeper .part.*/row-shape precision gap not closed by #329).

RuntimeContextSnapshot['agentOutputs'] carries weaker typing than runtime-side RuntimeAgentOutputObservation: Codex test needed asRecord defensive casts to read event.part.delta / event.part.name from snapshot rows, while same fields are properly typed when consumed from RuntimeOutputTable.events.rows() directly.

Next action: scope a client-sdk snapshot-observation typing fix; architectural (public observable type change like TFIND-030) — SDD/framing-gated.

title: TFIND-052: CONFIGS coverage denominator too coarse
description:
Area: tiny-firegrid / coverage tooling.

Status: open — cat-4 coverage-tooling (toy/tooling scope, NOT a production sidecar).

Triage: cat-4 (rubric coverage-tool-artifact — misleading denominator; resolution = improve the coverage script: a scenario-specific delta or outside-spine denominator; retain current thresholds only as a gross guardrail). Toy/tooling scope.

Drift sweep 2026-05-18: all 5 landed production-consuming configs report identical coverage (host_surface_closure 80.4% / end_to_end 86.6%) — once a config imports FiregridRuntimeHostLive + client SDK the closure denominator is too coarse to distinguish scenario-specific production surface. CONFIGS coverage must NOT be read as per-scenario surface proof.

title: TFIND-044: DurableTableProvider cannot carry N heterogeneous precise identities
description:
Area: client-sdk / effect-durable-operators provider.

Status: resolved (#348 798821692 — Option B named coarse provider seam, branded AnyDurableTableTag; SDD #345; signed off Gurdas 2026-05-18).

Triage: cat-2 (real boundary/wrong-shape — a real flamecast consumer hits it; the provider abstraction can't express the precise heterogeneous identity set; architect-gated; framing = §0 amendment to existing provider SDD).

DurableTableProvider's single ROut generic cannot carry N heterogeneous precise DurableTable <Self> identities (firegridRuntimeTableTags; flamecast client/main.tsx:360 TS2322) — a latent precision leak the TFIND-005 any was hiding. The provider API/usage WAS SDD'd (REACT_LIVE_QUERY); its generic type shape was not.

Resolution: §0 amendment to SDD_DURABLE_TABLE_REACT_LIVE_QUERY.md adding generic-identity type-shape decision; Option B = localized named coarse aggregate (branded AnyDurableTableTag) at the one provider seam, while every table stays precise everywhere else.

Co-gated #326 flip with TFIND-045/050.
close_reason: Resolved by #348 — Option B named coarse provider seam (branded AnyDurableTableTag).

title: TFIND-025: Durable-tools Shape C / wait arbitration not modeled (production-deferred)

Triage: cat-4/toy WRAPPER over a cat-1/2-FUTURE kernel (Shape C step 2). Step 1 LANDED; steps 2-3 verified architecturally DEFINED (convergence.md:74-81 — Step 2: collapse match/timeout arbitration onto DurableDeferred.raceAll since race deferred already decides + completions reads redundant given idempotent deferredDone; Step 3: delete dead completions table + reduce durable-wait-store.ts) ⇒ deterministic implementation, NOT undefined/framing-gated.

Precondition (per-context engine slice) MET (TFIND-010). = schedulable production cleanup ('recommended near-term'), NOT a bundle item. Toy modeling deferred until built — not toy laziness.

title: TFIND-049: Runtime host has no agent-adapter integration (Slice 4 unbuilt)
description:
Area: runtime host / agent-adapter integration.

Status: open — ARCHITECT/ROADMAP (build effect-ai-native-agents Slice 4? — Gurdas binary).

Triage: cat-4/toy WRAPPER over a cat-1/2-FUTURE kernel. Toy half (cat-4): toy correctly refuses to model a capability that does not exist. Kernel (cat-1/2): adapter-driven/AI-provider launch is deferred FUTURE production work (effect-ai-native-agents Slice 4), NOT a current-architecture gap/bug/regression — distinguish: nothing is broken today, the capability is planned-not-yet-built.

Verified evidence (file:line):
- RuntimeProviderSchema = Schema.Literal('local-process') — only value (protocol/src/launch/schema.ts:60).
- FiregridRuntimeHostLive imports + hardcodes LocalProcessSandboxProvider (host-sdk/src/host/layers.ts:31,143).
- Zero host-sdk consumers of AgentAdapterRegistry / adapterFor.
- docs/proposals/effect-ai-native-agents.md:474 — 'Slice 4: Runtime Host Integration — Wire AgentAdapterRegistry.adapterFor(context) into the runtime host'; explicitly future/unbuilt.

Surfaced 2026-05-18 by 33's Gurdas-directed agent-adapter-driven-pipeline build: it halted correctly per protocol rather than reach past / replace host internals. Migration-as-validation working exactly as designed.

Disposition = roadmap binary Gurdas owns (build Slice 4 vs. keep dequeued), NOT a coordinator/sidecar fix.

title: TFIND-003: No remote start request surface
description:
Area: client-sdk / host boundary.

Status: resolved (#332 — durable start request + host reconciler).

Triage: cat-1 (real remote client needs a durable start trigger, not an in-process host capability).

FiregridSessionHandle.start required RuntimeStartCapability, which is a host-process capability. There was no public client API that recorded 'start this session' as a durable control-plane request for a host to claim and execute. Resolved by the same #332 consolidated client/host transaction as TFIND-002.
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — durable RuntimeStartRequestRow + host reconciler.

title: TFIND-050: Provider seam ROut erasure — unknown breaks by-name path
description:
Area: effect-durable-operators / provider seam.

Status: open — corrective; ARCHITECTURAL decision (155 authoring §0 framing → architect signoff bundle). Gates #326 keystone.

Triage: cat-2 corrective (real defect shipped in resolved TFIND-044/#348; design-conformant fix — Option B intent = erase ROut at the seam, never is the correct erasure operator for a contravariant position, unknown was a #348 oversight; NOT an A/B reopen).

Merged #348 (signed-off TFIND-044 Option B) typed the erased provider prop layer: Layer.Layer<unknown, E, never> — Layer ROut is contravariant, so unknown <: <Table> fails on the explicit-props/by-name path (only the flamecast JSX-inference path ever worked). Latent type defect surfaced during the #326 rebase by 155, which HALTED (no paper).

Original fix attempt = unknown → never at the react.ts provider seam was falsified: 155 proved never relocates the breakage, doesn't cleanly fix both consumer paths. Now an architectural layer-erasure design decision, not a 2-line correction.

Process note: full-CI-green didn't catch it — no test referenced the props type by name on the failing path; latent until #326's by-name rebase.

title: TFIND-041: ToolUse event lifecycle is under-discriminated (decision B)
description:
Area: runtime / agent-event contract.

Status: resolved (#336, e361d4147 — decision B + by-decision doc-comment).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — real architectural shape question; resolution = right-sized by-decision doc-comment, zero behavior change).

ToolUse is normalized as a shared AgentOutputEvent, but execution authority is not carried by the event. ACP and stdio-jsonl both emit ToolUse, while workflow core interprets it by consulting codec/session mode.

DECIDED (Gurdas 2026-05-18): (B) Session-mode authority, made explicit. Session/codec mode owns the ToolUse execution lifecycle by decision, not by default. The canonical decision is now recorded in-code (#336, doc-comment at the agentProtocol === 'acp' branch): ACP = observation-only, stdio-jsonl = client-result roundtrip, the AgentOutput ToolUse event is deliberately NOT discriminated by execution authority, and the (A) event-level discriminant is a tracked, deliberately-deferred future option.
close_reason: Resolved by #336 — decision B + by-decision doc-comment landed.

title: TFIND-015: Permission flow and codec authority architectural decision
description:
Area: permissions / codecs.

Status: B IMPLEMENTED — #350 coordinator-APPROVED (2026-05-18); merge-gated on full GitHub CI green only.

Triage: cat-1/2 production architectural (resolved-direction: B). ACP codec currently does durable-deferred authority inside the codec (verified) vs README 'codecs may not own durable permission state' — B aligns impl to documented contract; TFIND-041 authority-family precedent.

Framing (SDD #350, by 163, coordinator-reviewed 2026-05-18): §0 = Gurdas-accepted question verbatim. A = bless ACP codec-side durable-deferred authority; B (recommended) = strict observation + workflow-side durable resumption.

Evidence (coordinator-verified): ACP codec genuinely does durable-deferred permission authority from inside the codec — DurableDeferred.make (acp/index.ts:154-158), engine.deferredResult poll + DurableDeferred.done (:283-328), requiring WorkflowEngine/WorkflowInstance — while codecs README explicitly states codecs 'may not own durable permission state.' Concrete documented-contract contradiction; B aligns impl to existing contract.

Verified in-diff: ACP codec drops WorkflowEngine/DurableDeferred (import line removed, no re-add); workflow owns the bridge via existing runtimeInputDeferredFor(contextId,seq) (no new wait primitive); deterministic test has real blocked-pending point BEFORE response ingress + exact runtime-context/<contextId>/input/1 deferred + no sleep-race; no forcing/widening cast.

On merge → toy permission-flow-pipeline capability EXISTS → next dispatchable toy build.
close_reason: TFIND-015 Build B implemented + MERGED in PR #350 (5ecc20d53): ACP codec stripped of durable permission authority, workflow owns the bridge via existing runtimeInputDeferredFor, deterministic emit-then-wait test (real blocked-pending point + exact deferred, no sleep-race), no forcing cast, SDD signed-off. Coordinator diff-verified. Gurdas D1 ruling 2026-05-18 = done; no separate pending kernel.

title: TFIND-031: Shared DurableTable tag-family provision missing (root cause)
description:
Area: host/toolkit composition.

Status: resolved (#331, e48d82904).

Triage: cat-2 (real host-composition correctness — missing shared DurableTable tag-family provision, was masked by the TFIND-005 any).

Surfaced by TFIND-005. Initially filed as 4 separate leaks (TFIND-031..034); Agent 2's diagnosis (2026-05-17) showed they are ONE root cause: all four production boundaries plus test fallout leak the same tag family (RuntimeControlPlaneTable, RuntimeOutputTable, and four DurableWait* row tags). The any collapsed every consumer's requirements channel, so a single missing provision site in the host/toolkit composition manifested at 4 production type-boundaries + test boundaries.

Resolution: Option-Y execution-scoped self-containment — support layer required what it provided. Fix (NO cast): provide the SAME HostRuntimeObservationSubstrateLive reference INTO the executor while keeping it provideMerged into the workflow chain → Effect memoization = ONE shared store (workflow body + wait-router + tool executor); toolCallWorkflowSupportLayer got analogous self-containment.

A first sibling-Layer.merge attempt typechecked but broke TOOL_EXECUTOR_SEAM.2 schedule_me — caught and rejected by deterministic record→blocked→wake test, not forced green; corrected re-thread passes it. Full gate green; closes the TFIND-005 leak-stack root.
close_reason: Resolved by #331 — Option-Y execution-scoped self-containment; one root cause, four manifestations folded in.

The toy 'under-modeling' is a symptom of an unbuilt production capability, not toy laziness.
